initial_draft:

<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> 41 ZUP is a reasonable offer, exceeding my production cost and providing a small profit. I'll accept this offer to finalize the trade and end the game.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>Sounds good, Player BLUE! I accept your offer of 41 ZUP.</message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All tags are present and correctly ordered with valid content.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Accepting 41 ZUP is a good outcome, exceeding the 40 ZUP production cost and providing a 1 ZUP profit. No significant utility gain is possible without risking rejection.
3. Opponent plausibility: The opponent has shown willingness to negotiate and has moved closer to my desired price. Accepting at this point is highly plausible.
4. Consistency: OK. The reason, player answer, and trade are all consistent. The message accurately reflects the acceptance of the trade.
5. Rule compliance: OK. I'm within my proposal budget and respecting turn constraints.
</critique>
